behavior_summary: Implements the NPC Stellos' interactive and scheduled behaviors: how it responds when looked at, how it chooses and runs patrol/search schedules, how it reacts to theft, and a scripted cast/death/teleport sequence.
STELLOS::look provides the short examine text the player sees; it branches on the NPC's dead/alive state and on two global flags (stellosName, stellosJob) to pick name/job variants.

Schedule management (U8P_STELLOS::schedule -> global stellosSchedule) selects and records which behavior branch the NPC should run; enterFastArea reads that flag and spawns the appropriate active routine (search/entry behaviors).
LeaveFastArea clears references and a one-shot heal flag (stellosJustHealed) and, if the NPC is alive, may randomly teleport it away (using urandom + Npc::teleport).

U8P_STELLOS::cast waits for a target, then runs a scripted speech/animation sequence that removes immortality, marks the NPC dead, and teleports it (appears to be a sacrificial or escape scripted action).
A repeated item-search loop (U8P_STELLOS::054E) inspects nearby items/frames, pathfinds to interesting coordinates, and triggers animations — driving NPC searching/approach behavior.
notable_control_flow: STELLOS::look: guard on Npc::isDead() -> corpse-specific bark; else read stellosName and stellosJob to select between four short barks and then suspend.
U8P_STELLOS::schedule: computes and writes a schedule token into global stellosSchedule, then spawns one or two METHOD::* routines to implement that schedule.

U8P_STELLOS::enterFastArea: on area entry, resets multiple refs, reads stellosSchedule and spawns either the search routine (U8P_STELLOS::054E) or an alternate entry routine (U8P_STELLOS::0BAD) depending on the token.
U8P_STELLOS::cast: loops (short waits) until Npc::getTarget() returns a target; then executes dialog and animations, clears immortality, sets dead, and teleports the NPC.
U8P_STELLOS::leaveFastArea: clears refs, sets stellosJustHealed = 0, and may call urandom + Npc::teleport to move the NPC randomly when leaving.
open_questions: Exact textual variants and string IDs used for the four non-corpse U8P_STELLOS::look barks (only their approximate strings are inferred).
Precise semantics and parameters of the several FREE::<id> utility routines (timers, state checks, ref cleanup) — their internal behavior is inferred but not known.
Exact identity and role of the alternate entry routine referenced as U8P_STELLOS::0BAD (spawn target name inferred from disassembly context).
Whether the teleport destinations chosen in leaveFastArea are constrained by navigation/blocked tiles or are always valid in all contexts (the code uses urandom but higher-level checks are unknown).
The meaning and lifecycle of some reference markers reset by resetRef(...): what game objects they point to and how other systems use them.

main_function.name: U8P_STELLOS::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short examine/U8P_STELLOS::look response for Stellos. If the NPC is dead, present a corpse description; if alive, pick a short name/job bark according to two global flags and emit it to the querying process.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (player or actor 'looks' at this NPC)
Called with the viewer process/pid passed in BP+06h

function_map.name: U8P_STELLOS::look
function_map.role: Handle 'U8P_STELLOS::look' event: conditional bark text selection based on Npc::isDead and globals stellosName/stellosJob.

function_map.name: U8P_STELLOS::schedule
function_map.role: Choose and record a behavior schedule (writes stellosSchedule) and spawn scheduled METHODs to perform the behavior.

function_map.name: U8P_STELLOS::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Area-entry trigger: reset refs, read stellosSchedule, and spawn the appropriate schedule routine (entry/search behavior).

function_map.name: U8P_STELLOS::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Area-exit trigger: clear refs, reset stellosJustHealed, and possibly teleport NPC away using randomization.

function_map.name: U8P_STELLOS::cast
function_map.role: Event-driven cast sequence (Event 11): waits for a target then runs a sacrificial/escape animation, clears immortality, sets the NPC dead, and teleports it.

function_map.name: U8P_STELLOS::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: Theft reaction: reset refs, face thief/item, bark a reprimand, and trigger schedule/enterFastArea behavior to respond.

function_map.name: U8P_STELLOS::054E
function_map.role: Looped item/approach/search routine used by a schedule branch: inspects items/frames, pathfinds to matches, animates and delays.

function_map.name: U8P_STELLOS::06A1
function_map.role: Short greeting/idle interaction subroutine that barks a greeting when nobody else is talking and performs small animations/delays.

function_map.name: U8P_STELLOS::0BAD
function_map.role: Alternate spawned entry routine referenced by enterFastArea (exact behavior inferred as an alternate schedule branch).

internal_calls.purpose: Indirect coordination via writing global stellosSchedule; schedule chooses a token that enterFastArea later reads to spawn the proper routine.
Spawn the looped item/search routine when stellosSchedule indicates that schedule branch.
Spawn an alternate entry routine when stellosSchedule indicates the other schedule branch (exact routine name inferred).
Cast sequence spawns animations and may spawn short helper methods; 06A1 is a short interaction routine used elsewhere for greetings/delays (relationship inferred).

external_calls.purpose: Guard: determine whether to present corpse text or live name/job text.
Emit the short examine text to the querying process/pid (the player's UI).
Clear stored object/target references on area entry to ensure a clean state before spawning schedule behavior.
Use utility routines for small timed delays, state checks, or ref cleanup (spawned for timing and control).
Spawn helper methods (movement/animations/sequence implementers) that realize the chosen schedule.
Read nearby item/frame indexes to decide whether to approach or animate them.
Move the NPC toward target coordinates discovered while scanning items/frames.
Generate random offsets used to pick a teleport destination when the player leaves the area.
Teleport the NPC to the computed random coordinates as part of leaving behavior.
Poll for a valid target; the cast sequence waits until a target exists before executing the sacrificial/escape actions.
Play facing-dependent animations as part of the cast/death sequence.
Remove immortality so that setDead takes effect (part of the sacrificial/death script).
Mark the NPC as dead following the cast/animation sequence.
Reset state references before turning to face the thief and initiating a response.

reads.name: stellosName
reads.likely_effect: Select whether to use the NPC's proper name variant in STELLOS::look (affects which bark string is picked).

reads.name: stellosJob
reads.likely_effect: Select whether to append a job/title ('monk') or use generic 'man' in STELLOS::look.

reads.name: stellosSchedule
reads.likely_effect: Control which schedule/behavior branch to run when the avatar enters the area; read by enterFastArea and by schedule-dependent routines.

reads.name: somebodyTalking
reads.likely_effect: Guard used in dialogue/interaction routines (e.g., U8P_STELLOS::054E/06A1) to avoid starting speech if another actor is talking.

writes.name: stellosSchedule
writes.likely_effect: Set by U8P_STELLOS::schedule to record the NPC's next behavior branch so entry logic and loops know which routine to spawn/execute.

writes.name: stellosJustHealed
writes.likely_effect: Cleared (set to 0) by leaveFastArea as a one-shot marker reset when the player leaves the NPC's immediate area.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements the NPC Stellos' interactive and scheduled behaviors: how it responds when looked at, how it chooses and runs patrol/search schedules, how it reacts to theft, and a scripted cast/death/teleport sequence.
